items.title: Nozomi joins Anthropic's Project Glasswing on OT security
items.summary: AI-driven vulnerability hunting for power, water and transport systems is set to expand as Nozomi brings OT expertise to Anthropic's initiative.

items.title: Watchlist screening: Reducing false positives in Australia & New Zealand
items.summary: Higher false positive rates are slowing onboarding and draining compliance teams as sanctions screening volumes rise across Australia and New Zealand.

items.title: Zscaler flags Claude chat malware campaign on Macs
items.summary: Mac users were lured via Google ads and shared Claude chats into installing malware that steals passwords, files and wallet data.

items.summary: Security teams under pressure to patch faster can now track and stage BIG-IP updates across fleets without losing audit control.

items.summary: The preview could ease multi-region compliance headaches by letting organisations apply column-level security through global tags and regional policies.

items.title: Inc Ransomware Exploits SonicWall SMA Zero-Days
items.summary: When chained together, the two vulnerabilities allow threat actors to gain root-level capabilities on SonicWall's mobile access appliances.

items.title: ICE to Pay Thomson Reuters $125 Million to Find ‘Voter Fraud’
items.summary: A new procurement record says ICE wants access to personal data, including names, Social Security numbers, and ethnicity, to investigate in part what the agency calls “voters fraud.” It also plans to use the data to investigate immigration fraud.

items.summary: The White House launched Gold Eagle to coordinate vulnerability response in a new AI world, but multiple questions linger over how it's being implemented.

items.title: 1M+ Emails Use Hidden Text to Dupe AI Security Filters
items.summary: Artificial intelligence and LLMs can be surprisingly ineffective against text salting, allowing phishing emails to slide right into your inbox.
